body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

meta.title: System for Cross-domain Identity Management: Definitions, Overview, Concepts, and Requirements

interoperability and security so interoperability uh in that there are many many standards in the Enterprise world that are um sometimes even competing with each other open and saml for example um even when there is only one standard in use there uh the standard is often very flexible so think of how you can bend ooth in many interesting and creative ways uh and as well open connect many parts of the specs are optional um you can you can choose to customize things and add your own extension points and uh get very creative uh what this ends up meaning in practice is that a lot of times you will end up with Enterprise identity providers that have gone and done some of these custom things or only implemented parts of the specs that were optional and made people have made different decisions about which of the optional aspects to implement which results in a very high cost of developing against these implementations because you have to go and read the vendor's specific documentation rather than following the standard on the security side uh again because these Sanders are so flexible there's often insecure configurations of them insecure uses of them um for For Better or For Worse sometimes those are okay given a certain threat model and sometimes they're not okay um but uh beyond that there's also there's just a lot of things are changing now in the world and there are a lot of new threats coming and there are a lot of new Solutions needed to solve those um we got very far in fighting a lot of these threats with single sign on alone and we have we've now passed that we've crossed that bridge and that is no longer enough to combat the modern threat landscape in Enterprise identity uh in other words it password hacking is like password just you know raw password attacks are not the place people are breaking in the front door anymore people are going in through very creative ways that need a lot of new Creative Solutions um so that is kind of the problem space that the Ipsy working group is working in uh the scope of the charter of the op Foundation working group is um these elements addressing single sign on entitlements life cycle management there there's a lot of um existing things that are deployed and working fine uh the goal is to reduce the options available and remove the insecure options uh choose the secure defaults make it more interoperable reduce the ambiguity make it so that um implementers can actually follow the spe rather than follow vendor documentation so this brings me to how this relates to uh to skim um this is a
